summary: Sprint 9 changes are performance-neutral to slightly positive. The portfolios.py split reduces module load time by removing unused imports from the main portfolios module. The DashboardMetrics.tsx and PortfolioList.tsx split does not introduce new queries or re-renders — props flow down from the parent query. The analytics.py shim is 16 lines and has zero overhead. ErrorBoundary wrapping in analytics/page.tsx adds negligible React overhead. The generateSparklineData function in DashboardMetrics.tsx uses Math.random() on every render which could cause unnecessary re-renders if the parent re-renders — this is low severity as it existed before in page.tsx.

findings.title: generateSparklineData called on every render in DashboardMetrics
findings.detail: generateSparklineData uses Math.random() so memoization would not help, but it runs on every parent re-render. This was the same in the original page.tsx — not a regression.
findings.fix: Consider moving sparkline generation to parent component as a derived constant, or accept it as acceptable cost (7 data points, no network calls).
